# SchemaShield - JSON Schema Validator

## Overview

SchemaShield is a secure interpreter for JSON Schema (draft-06, draft-07) that prioritizes architectural stability and developer experience over raw synthetic throughput. Designed for strict environments and complex domain logic.

## Key Differentiators

- **Zero Code Generation**: Fully CSP-compliant, no eval() or new Function()
- **Stack-Safe**: Flat loop architecture minimizes stack usage, no stack overflow on deeply nested data
- **Runtime Object Validation**: Native support for validating class instances, Dates, and custom objects alongside serialized JSON
- **Built-in Error Trees**: Advanced error reporting with getCause(), getTree(), getPath() methods
- **Zero Dependencies**: No external runtime dependencies

## Benchmarks

- **Bun**: #1 fastest validator at 11,740 ops/s — 2.5x faster than ajv, 4x faster than schemasafe
- **Node.js**: #3 fastest at 13,161 ops/s (69.8% ajv) — 50x faster than jsonschema
- **Zero Compilation Overhead**: Ideal for serverless/edge cold starts

## Supported Features

- **JSON Schema Drafts**: 06, 07
- **Keywords**: allOf, anyOf, oneOf, $ref, $id, required, properties, additionalProperties, enum, const, uniqueItems, pattern, patternProperties, minLength, maxLength, minimum, maximum, type, format, and more
- **Custom Extensions**:
  - addType(name, validatorFn) — custom type validators
  - addFormat(name, validatorFn) — custom format validators
  - addKeyword(name, validatorFn) — custom keyword validators

## Use Cases

- **Secure Environments**: Strict CSP policies where eval() is banned
- **Serverless/Edge**: Cloudflare Workers, Vercel Edge Functions, Deno Deploy, Bun
- **Complex Domain Validation**: Unifying DTO validation with business rules
- **Debugging Priority**: Clean stack traces, no black-box generated code

## Installation

```bash
npm install schema-shield
# or
bun add schema-shield
```

## Basic Usage

```javascript
import { SchemaShield } from "schema-shield";

const validator = new SchemaShield().compile({
  type: "object",
  properties: {
    name: { type: "string" },
    email: { type: "string", format: "email" }
  },
  required: ["name"]
});

validator({ name: "John", email: "john@example.com" });
// { valid: true, data: { name: "John", email: "john@example.com" } }

validator({ email: "invalid" });
// { valid: false, error: ValidationError }
```

### Serverless / Edge Deployment

SchemaShield is ideal for serverless and edge environments due to zero compilation overhead:

```javascript
// Compile at module level - runs once at cold start
const validateRequest = new SchemaShield().compile({
  type: "object",
  properties: {
    name: { type: "string" },
    email: { type: "string", format: "email" }
  },
  required: ["name", "email"]
});

// Vercel Edge / Cloudflare Worker handler
export default function handler(request) {
  const result = validateRequest(request.body);
  
  if (!result.valid) {
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: result.error.message }), {
      status: 400,
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" }
    });
  }
  
  return new Response(JSON.stringify(result.data), { status: 200 });
}
```

### Sharing Compiled Validators

Compiled validators are safe to share across requests:

```javascript
// Compile once at startup
const validateUser = new SchemaShield().compile(userSchema);

// Reuse across requests - no mutable state
app.get("/user/:id", (req) => {
  const result = validateUser(req.body);
  // ...
});
```

## Interpreter Architecture

Unlike JIT compilers that generate code at runtime, SchemaShield uses a flat-loop interpreter:

1. **Compilation**: Schema is compiled into a tree of validator functions at compile-time
2. **Reference Resolution**: $ref links are resolved once during compilation
3. **Validation**: Data flows through the validator tree in a flat loop (no recursion)

### Performance Characteristics

| Schema Type | Performance | Notes |
|-------------|-------------|-------|
| Simple (type, properties) | Very fast | ~same as JIT |
| allOf/anyOf/oneOf | ~70% JIT | Sequential branch evaluation |
| Deep nesting | Stack-safe | Constant memory, no recursion |
| Many $refs | Fast | Resolved at compile-time |

### Performance Optimization Tips

```javascript
// Best: Flat structure
const fastSchema = {
  type: "object",
  properties: {
    name: { type: "string" },
    email: { type: "string", format: "email" }
  }
};

// Slower: Deeply nested allOf/anyOf
const slowSchema = {
  allOf: [
    { allOf: [{ allOf: [...] }]}
  ]
};

// Better: Flatten your schema
const betterSchema = {
  type: "object",
  properties: { /* ... */ }
};
```

## API Reference

### Constructor Options

```javascript
new SchemaShield({ immutable?: boolean, failFast?: boolean })
```

- **immutable**: Set to true to prevent modifications to input data during validation
- **failFast**: Set to false to receive detailed error objects (default: true)

### Methods

- `compile(schema)` — Compiles a JSON Schema into a validator function
- `addType(name, validatorFn, overwrite?)` — Adds a custom type validator
- `addFormat(name, validatorFn, overwrite?)` — Adds a custom format validator
- `addKeyword(name, validatorFn, overwrite?)` — Adds a custom keyword validator
- `getType(name)` — Retrieves a custom type validator
- `getFormat(name)` — Retrieves a custom format validator
- `getKeyword(name)` — Retrieves a custom keyword validator

### Validation Result

```typescript
{
  valid: boolean,
  data?: any,      // validated data (with defaults applied)
  error?: ValidationError | true  // error details or true in failFast mode
}
```

### ValidationError Methods

- `getPath()` — Returns { schemaPath, instancePath } as JSON Pointers
- `getTree()` — Returns full error chain as nested object
- `getCause()` — Returns the root cause of the error

## Comparison with Other Validators

| Feature              | SchemaShield | ajv  | schemasafe |
|---------------------|-------------|------|-----------|
| CSP-compliant       | Native      | No   | Yes       |
| Stack-safe          | Flat loop   | No   | No        |
| Class instances     | Native      | No   | No        |
| Zero dependencies   | Yes         | No   | Yes       |
| Built-in formats    | 20+         | No   | Yes       |
| Bun performance     | #1 fastest  | 40%  | 25%       |
| Node.js performance | #3 fastest  | 100% | 74%       |

## Limitations

- **No Remote $ref**: Security by design — schemas must be bundled locally
- **Unicode Length**: minLength/maxLength use UTF-16 code units, not Unicode codepoints
- **Draft-07 Scope**: Limited dynamic $id scope resolution in nested sub-schemas

## Security Philosophy

SchemaShield follows a Zero Trust and Hermetic Architecture approach:
- No network access at runtime
- Deterministic validation (no external dependencies)
- Treat schemas as code dependencies, not dynamic assets

### CSP-Compliant Validation

SchemaShield works in strict CSP environments because it uses no code generation:

```javascript
// No eval(), no new Function() - CSP-safe
const validator = new SchemaShield().compile({
  type: "object",
  properties: {
    name: { type: "string" }
  }
});

// This works in strict CSP policies
const result = validator({ name: "John" });
```

### Preventing Prototype Pollution

SchemaShield prevents prototype pollution by design:

```javascript
const schema = {
  type: "object",
  properties: {
    name: { type: "string" }
  },
  additionalProperties: false
};

const validator = new SchemaShield().compile(schema);

// Malicious input {"__proto__": {"evil": "value"}} will fail
validator({ "__proto__": { "evil": "value" } });
// { valid: false, error: ValidationError }
```

### Immutable Mode

Protect input data from accidental modification:

```javascript
const shield = new SchemaShield({ immutable: true });
const validator = shield.compile({
  type: "object",
  properties: {
    name: { type: "string" }
  }
});

const input = { name: "John" };
const result = validator(input);

// Input remains unchanged
console.log(input);
// { name: "John" }
```
